title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
summary: The talk presents a four-layer architecture for sustaining brand voice in AI products: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. This approach is contrasted with single-pass tone instructions, using examples from wedding venue, companion AI, and missing persons tools.
writeup: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for maintaining brand voice in AI products: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and a deterministic post-generation veto. The approach is set against single-pass tone instructions, which the talk treats as insufficient for sustaining a consistent voice. The layers separate what never changes from what shifts by situation, ground voice in examples rather than abstract adjectives, and add a rule-based filter after generation to catch outputs that violate the voice. The method is illustrated with cases drawn from a wedding venue, a companion AI, and a missing persons tool.
